rawCommits.subject: fix(feed): use commitIds as fallback count in PushCardBody
rawCommits.body:
When metadata.commits is absent (older posts), PushCardBody now
falls back to commitIds.length for the displayed commit count
instead of showing 0. Also adds a test for partial metadata.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: use commitIds as fallback count in PushCardBody

rawCommits.subject: Harden changelog media proof capture
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Harden changelog media proof capture

rawCommits.subject: fix(feed): center leaderboard and remove stray horizontal scrollbar
rawCommits.body:
LeaderboardContent wrapped rows in a nested scroll container
(.container + .chartWrap) inside FeedMainPanel's .scrollArea. Because
.chartWrap used overflow-y: auto without hiding scrollbars, and added
an extra var(--space-5) of horizontal padding, the centering calc on
.chartInner (designed to sit directly in .scrollArea) pushed content
past the available width and surfaced a horizontal scrollbar.

Drop the nested wrappers and render rows directly inside a single
centered .list, matching FeedMainPanel.feedList. Scrolling, padding
and scrollbar hiding are already handled by the parent .scrollArea.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: center leaderboard and remove stray horizontal scrollbar

rawCommits.subject: feat(debug): project-first nav, sidekick-driven detail view
rawCommits.body:
- Replace bespoke DebugNav folder sections with the shared LeftMenuTree so
  every project (including those with no recorded runs) appears at the top
  level, matching the Projects/Process apps.
- Move the run toolbar, counters, and entry inspector out of the middle
  panel into a new sidekick (tabs: Run, Events, LLM, Iterations, Blockers,
  Retries, Stats, Tasks). Clicking an event row in the timeline now drives
  the inspector via a debug-sidekick-store, mirroring the Process pattern.
- Build a portal-backed DebugFilterMenu for the type filter so dropdowns
  aren't clipped by the main lane anymore.
- Unwrap the `{_ts, event}` JSONL envelope written by loop_log so rows no
  longer show "unknown" for type/timestamp.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: project-first nav, sidekick-driven detail view

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): surface agent-busy state when automation is running
rawCommits.body:
When the automation loop is running a task, the upstream harness
rejects any new `UserMessage` on the same agent with "A turn is
currently in progress; send cancel first" -- and the chat input
doesn't switch to the stop icon, leaving the user with no way to
cancel from the chat surface.

- Combine chat SSE streaming and loop-active state via a new
  `useAgentBusy` hook so the chat input shows the stop icon
  whenever the agent is busy, regardless of the source.
- Route `onStop` to `/loop/stop` when the loop (not chat SSE) is
  holding the turn, so the stop button actually cancels what's
  running.
- Guard `POST /agents/:id/events/stream` with a typed `409
  agent_busy` when the automaton registry shows a live, unpaused
  run for the agent instance, and render it as a friendly message
  instead of echoing the raw harness string.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: surface agent-busy state when automation is running

rawCommits.subject: style(ui): calm borders on live task output and run output blocks
rawCommits.body:
Swap `var(--color-border)` for the subtler `var(--color-border-light)`
on the Preview `.taskStream` / `.buildOutput` containers and the
Process run's per-event timeline cards so they match the calmer border
used inside the main LLM chat section.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(ui): calm borders on live task output and run output blocks

rawCommits.subject: fix(debug): mount sidekick for Debug app and stabilize run-detail header
rawCommits.body:
The Debug app already exports SidekickPanel/SidekickTaskbar, but the
registry entry omitted the hasSidekickPanel/hasSidekickTaskbar flags, so
the shell never mounted the right-hand lane and clicking a run only
updated the middle panel. Register both flags so selecting a run in the
sidebar or grid now populates the sidekick as intended.

Also stabilize the run-detail header: while metadata loaded, the title
went from "Run" to "Run . <started>" and the subtitle from "loading" to
"<status> . ended <date>", which caused the wrap-enabled header to
reflow and the second line to jump on arrival. Render a stable
"Run . -" placeholder, always include the ended segment, reserve
min-width/min-height on the title column, and switch the header to
nowrap so the channel label can no longer reflow underneath.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: mount sidekick for Debug app and stabilize run-detail header

rawCommits.subject: feat(debug): copy-all and export buttons in run-detail header
rawCommits.body:
Add "Copy all" / "Copy filtered" and "Export" buttons to the top of the
run-detail view so operators can grab the current channel's JSONL or a
full run bundle without hopping to the Run tab in the sidekick. Copy
follows the visible timeline: full channel JSONL when no filter is
active, otherwise the joined filtered rows. Export reuses the existing
exportRunBlob endpoint and downloads debug-<project>-<run>.zip.

Extract the previously-duplicated copyToClipboard and downloadBlob
helpers into a shared apps/debug/clipboard.ts and switch RunInfoTab
and EntryInspector to the shared module so we don't keep copies
drifting.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: copy-all and export buttons in run-detail header

rawCommits.subject: fix(debug): tighten run-detail header and align inspector actions
rawCommits.body:
Collapse the two-line run title + status subtitle into a single line
("Run . <started> . <status>"), drop the redundant "ended <date>"
segment, and hide the "All events" label on the default channel since
the sidekick tabs already indicate what is showing. Match the header
height to the sidebar search input (--control-height-sm, 32px) and
promote its underline to the solid --color-border so the top bar has
the same visual weight as the search field above it.

Also left-align the EntryInspector's "Copy JSON" / "Close" buttons with
the field labels above them -- .inspectorActions was applying an extra
horizontal padding on top of the parent .taskMeta padding, so the
actions were pushed one --space-3 to the right.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: tighten run-detail header and align inspector actions

rawCommits.subject: Phase 0: fix loop_log counter double-count and add narration_deltas
rawCommits.body:
- update_counters only sums input_tokens/output_tokens on
  assistant_message_end or usage.final=true, ignoring mid-stream
  token_usage frames that inflated per-turn counts.
- Add RunCounters.narration_deltas (incremented on text_delta events)
  as a first-class signal for zero-tool-calls / narration-bloat
  heuristics so consumers don't re-scan events.jsonl.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 0: fix loop_log counter double-count and add narration_deltas

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): decode ANSI-colored tool output in task panel
rawCommits.body:
Task output from colored CLIs (cargo, rustc, npm, ...) was rendering as
raw base64 in the task section because `tryDecodeBase64` bailed whenever
the decoded bytes contained `\u001B` (ESC, 0x1B) — the first byte of
every ANSI CSI/OSC escape. Allow ESC through, strip ANSI escapes from
the decoded text, and expand `decodeBase64Fields` to recurse and to
cover additional output keys (`output`, `text`, `content`, `log`).
Also require the base64 length to be a multiple of 4 so non-base64
strings that happen to use the base64 charset pass through unchanged.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: decode ANSI-colored tool output in task panel

rawCommits.subject: Phase 2a: add RemediationHint to heuristic findings
rawCommits.body:
- Extend Finding with an optional RemediationHint enum covering
  split-write, reshape-search, force-tool-call, retry-smaller-scope,
  and no-auto-fix variants.
- Each existing rule now populates a hint that matches its failure
  mode (e.g. zero_tool_calls_in_turn -> ForceToolCallNextTurn,
  task_never_completed / repeated_blocker_path -> SplitWrite...).
- aura-run-analyze renders the hint as a compact one-liner under
  each finding so dev loops inherit actionable next steps.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 2a: add RemediationHint to heuristic findings

rawCommits.subject: style(ui): use darker block border on run/task overlay output sections
rawCommits.body:
Swap the lighter --color-border-light stroke for the standard --color-border
token on the run event timeline rows and the task live/build output blocks so
they match the .block primitive outline.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(ui): use darker block border on run/task overlay output sections

rawCommits.subject: Phase 3: auto-decompose or reshape tasks after truncation failures
rawCommits.body:
When a task fails with a truncation/no-file-ops reason, load the run
bundle, run heuristics, and act on the first RemediationHint:
 - SplitWriteIntoSkeletonPlusAppends -> two child tasks (skeleton + fill)
 - ReshapeSearchQuery                 -> one shaped-retry task
 - ForceToolCallNextTurn              -> one shaped-retry task

All three paths transition the parent task to failed and broadcast a
task_auto_remediated domain event so the UI can render the chain.
Honors MAX_RETRIES_PER_TASK and falls back to the existing retry
path when the env flag AURA_AUTO_DECOMPOSE_DISABLED=1 is set.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 3: auto-decompose or reshape tasks after truncation failures

rawCommits.subject: style(ui): match chat border token inside sidekick and preview overlays
rawCommits.body:
Propagate the main chat's darker --color-border override (#17171a) from
ChatPanel.module.css to the sidekick body and the preview overlay so
tables, blocks, tools, and output sections inside the sidekick render
with the same subtle outline as the LLM chat.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(ui): match chat border token inside sidekick and preview overlays

rawCommits.subject: Phase 5: preemptively decompose oversized task specs at ingestion
rawCommits.body:
detect_preflight_decomposition() flags tasks whose title/description
signal a likely-oversized generation (full-implementation phrases,
long specs with write hints, explicit large line counts). Matching
tasks are split into [skeleton] + [fill] children at creation time,
the parent is moved to a non-runnable status, and a
task_preflight_decomposed domain event is broadcast.

The skeleton+fill fan-out is extracted into spawn_skeleton_and_fill_
children(), now shared with Phase 3's post-failure path. Honors both
AURA_AUTO_DECOMPOSE_DISABLED and a new per-task skip_auto_decompose
opt-out flag.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 5: preemptively decompose oversized task specs at ingestion

rawCommits.subject: Phase 6: run heuristics live against the in-progress bundle
rawCommits.body:
Add LiveAnalyzer that re-runs aura_run_heuristics::analyze every 50
events, every 30s, or immediately on task_failed during an active run,
and broadcasts new findings as dev_loop_advisory / heuristic_finding
domain events (with RemediationHint payload) so the UI can surface
them mid-flight.

Findings are deduped within a run via their stable ids, filtered to
Warn/Error severity, and respect the AURA_LIVE_HEURISTICS_DISABLED
env opt-out. Phase 3's post-failure path continues to be the
authoritative actor on hints; this phase is observational.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 6: run heuristics live against the in-progress bundle

rawCommits.subject: Phase 7: replay integration and golden tests for autonomous recovery
rawCommits.body:
7a. New integration test apps/aura-os-server/tests/autonomous_recovery
    _replay.rs builds a synthetic run bundle that reproduces the
    original write_file truncation failure and asserts the full
    decision chain: classify_failure -> Truncation, heuristics
    -> SplitWriteIntoSkeletonPlusAppends, detect_preflight_
    decomposition -> Some.

7b. New golden test in aura-run-analyze pins the rendered output
    (including Phase 2a's `fix:` remediation lines) against a
    committed fixture. UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 regenerates the expected
    file.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Phase 7: replay integration and golden tests for autonomous recovery

rawCommits.subject: style(ui): unify --color-border token across sidekick, tools, blocks, and previews
rawCommits.body:
Promote the darker #17171a border token to the global :root so tables, blocks, tool rows, message bubbles, preview overlays, task/terminal panels, and sidekick surfaces all share the same subtle outline as the main LLM chat. Drops the lighter rgba(255,255,255,0.0x) fallbacks in border declarations so the global token always wins, and removes the now-redundant per-container --color-border overrides on ChatPanel, PreviewOverlay, and Sidekick.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style(ui): unify --color-border token across sidekick, tools, blocks, and previews

rawCommits.subject: chore(harness): drop uncommitted AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN bearer path
rawCommits.body:
Removes the in-progress shared-secret bearer token plumbing between
aura-os and the harness (aura-node). All of the following were
working-tree-only additions that never landed; this commit marks the
decision to abandon the concept:

- HarnessHttpGateway.auth_token / with_auth / apply_auth
- AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN env read + warn in app_builder
- --external-harness AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN warning in desktop main
- LocalHarness.auth_token / with_auth_token / env_auth_token /
  WS Authorization header injection

User JWT flow (SessionConfig.token, AutomatonStartParams.auth_token,
HarnessClient bearer forwarding, auth_guard, .bearer_auth() to zOS /
aura-network / aura-storage / integrations / billing, ToolAuth::Bearer)
is untouched. AutomatonClient.auth_token and SwarmHarness.auth_token
fields remain in place but are now permanently None since nothing
populates them.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: drop uncommitted AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN bearer path

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): decode base64 stdout in command and read_file blocks
rawCommits.body:
Command blocks (run_command) and read_file blocks were rendering the
raw tool-result envelope - leaving captured output as unreadable
base64 text and (for read_file) showing the whole JSON envelope on
one logical line inside the code viewer. The transport intentionally
base64-encodes stdout/stderr for binary-safe JSON; the shared
decoder in format.ts already handles this for GenericToolBlock but
was not used by these two specialized renderers.

- Export tryDecodeBase64 and add decodeCapturedOutput in format.ts
  to parse the {ok, stdout, stderr, exit_code, metadata} envelope
  and decode captured output via the existing ANSI-stripping,
  control-char-guarded decoder.
- CommandBlock: replace local parseCommandResult with the shared
  helper so stdout/stderr render as legible text.
- FileBlock: parse the read_file envelope and feed decoded stdout
  to CodeView so the file contents render syntax-highlighted by
  path instead of a raw JSON blob. Fall back to an inline error row
  when ok=false.
- Tests: add decodeCapturedOutput unit tests and ToolRow render
  tests asserting base64 is decoded, ANSI escapes stripped, and the
  raw envelope never appears in the DOM for command/read_file.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: decode base64 stdout in command and read_file blocks

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): extract rows from base64 stdout envelopes in ListBlock
rawCommits.body:
list_files, find_files and search_code (Codex-provided MCP tools)
return their payload as a newline-separated listing that is base64-
encoded inside the tool envelope's `stdout` field, not as a bare
JSON array. ListBlock's extractRows only walked the top-level of the
parsed JSON, so the envelope yielded zero rows and the UI always
rendered "0 items / No results" for these tools.

- Add resolveRows() that first tries the existing bare-JSON path
  (preserving behavior for native list_specs/list_tasks/etc.), then
  falls back to decodeCapturedOutput and treats stdout as JSON or,
  if that fails, newline-separated plain text.
- Add rowsFromTextOutput() that understands `path:line: match` lines
  from search_code, splitting them into a primary (file:line) and
  secondary (match preview) column so long matches do not overflow.
- Tests cover find_files, list_files and search_code envelope paths
  in addition to the existing bare-JSON case.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: extract rows from base64 stdout envelopes in ListBlock

rawCommits.subject: feat(billing): lock billing email to ZERO account identity
rawCommits.body:
The org billing email is auto-provisioned from the ZERO auth identity
and must stay in sync with that account. A recent regression let users
edit it from the Team Settings panel, which booted them into a Free-plan
state when the edit did not match their ZERO record.

- Drop billing_email from SetBillingRequest and preserve the existing
  stored value in set_billing, so stale clients that still send it get
  their payload silently discarded.
- Render the Billing Email row as read-only text with a "Tied to your
  ZERO account" caption; remove the Input + Save button and the
  onBillingEmailChange / saving / onSave props from OrgSettingsBilling.
- Trim setBillingEmail / handleSaveBilling out of useOrgSettingsData and
  simplify api.orgs.setBilling to (orgId, plan) only.
- Update unit tests for the new read-only UI and { plan }-only PUT body.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: lock billing email to ZERO account identity

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): restore 10px left inset on GenericToolBlock result containers
rawCommits.body:
The generic tool block (used for task_done and any unregistered tool)
rendered its Input/Status/Result payloads flush against the block's
left edge with no border, instead of aligning with the 10px header
inset that every other renderer uses.

Root cause: Block.module.css intentionally strips styles from <pre>
elements nested inside a body (`.blockBody.blockBody pre { padding:0;
margin:0; border:none; background:transparent }`) so markdown-
rendered prose pres cannot bleed ancestor padding into renderer
content. That reset's specificity (0,3,1) also beat `.genericJson`
(0,1,0), which used `padding: 8px 10px; margin: 0 10px; border` to
draw a padded JSON viewer.

Fix: render the JSON containers as <div>s instead of <pre>s in
GenericToolBlock. The `.genericJson` class already sets
`white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-all`, so the visual
formatting is identical while the reset no longer applies. SpecBlock
(which deliberately uses an unclassed <pre> inside .codeArea to
inherit the reset) is left untouched.

Add a regression test that renders an unknown `task_done` tool and
asserts no <pre> survives in the body and both Input + Result
containers are <div>s carrying the `.genericJson` class.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: restore 10px left inset on GenericToolBlock result containers

rawCommits.subject: refactor(ui): linear stream rendering, markdown safety, writing-aware cooking indicator
rawCommits.body:
Three long-standing issues in the live LLM stream all shared the same
root cause: handleTextDelta folded trailing text back into the earliest
text timeline item across intervening tool/thinking blocks, and the
indicator layer could not distinguish "actively writing" from "stalled
mid-flow". Untangling these simplifies the whole streaming surface.

- handleTextDelta now strictly appends to the timeline tail. A text
  chunk extends the last item if it is text, otherwise a fresh text
  item is pushed after the last tool/thinking block. needsSeparator,
  the spec-tool flushStreamingText, and the mergingAcrossNonText
  paragraph-break logic are all gone. Arrival order == render order,
  live and historical.
- getStreamSafeContent handles trailing dangling * / _ markers, lone
  trailing emphasis runs, and odd emphasis counts in the last prose
  block while leaving balanced markers and inline code alone. No more
  raw ** or * flashing under the cursor while the closer is in flight.
- The stream store now tracks isWriting (displayedTextLength less than
  streamBuffer.length). getStreamingPhaseLabel hides the cooking
  indicator only while words are actively revealing; it stays visible
  for thinking, pending tools, post-tool consolidation, and inter-chunk
  stalls.

Tests updated accordingly; new unit coverage for linear ordering across
tools, emphasis edge cases, and phase-label gating.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: linear stream rendering, markdown safety, writing-aware cooking indicator

rawCommits.subject: fix(auth): stop logout landing on a black-screen redirect loop
rawCommits.body:
Logout previously produced a black screen that only a manual wipe of the
on-disk SettingsStore could recover from. Two interacting bugs drove it:

1) `App.tsx` computed `initiallyLoggedIn = isLoggedInSync()` once at
   module load and OR-ed it into `showShell` forever. After a logout
   `user` flipped to `null` but `initiallyLoggedIn` stayed `true`, so
   `showShell` stuck at `true`. `RequireAuth` then redirected to `/login`,
   the `/login` route saw `showShell === true` and `<Navigate to="/" />`-
   ed back, producing an infinite redirect.

2) `logout()` ended with `window.location.href = "/login"`, triggering a
   full reload. The desktop initialization script carries auth literals
   baked at app startup (see `build_initialization_script` in
   `apps/aura-os-desktop/src/main.rs`) and re-runs on every navigation,
   re-writing the just-cleared `aura-session` / `aura-jwt` keys and
   redefining `__AURA_BOOT_AUTH__` as logged-in — resurrecting the dead
   session exactly on the reload that was supposed to end it. And if
   `authApi.logout()` threw (stale token, offline), none of the local
   cleanup ran at all.

Fixes:

- Gate the `App.tsx` boot-time flag: `showShell` now equals
  `isAuthenticated || (initiallyLoggedIn && !hasResolvedInitialSession)`.
  The boot snapshot only suppresses the first-render flash for returning
  users; once the auth store has resolved its initial session the live
  state is authoritative.
- Drop the `window.location.href` reload from `logout()` and wrap the
  server call in a try/catch so local cleanup (IDB, localStorage, event
  socket, store) always runs. React Router now flips to `LoginView`
  in-place via the new `showShell` gate.
- Introduce a sticky `aura-force-logged-out` sentinel via the new
  `endLocalSession()` helper. Set on user-initiated logout and on any
  401 that invalidates the session, and cleared by `setStoredAuth()`
  when a real session is persisted. `seedCachedSessionFromBoot()` and
  `hydrateStoredAuth()` honour the sentinel: even if a reload re-runs
  the stale-baked desktop init script, the sentinel wipes the ghost
  mirrors and forces the app to come up on `LoginView`.
- `clearStoredAuth()` now also wipes the `aura-idb:auth:session`
  localStorage fallback, which the browser-db layer uses to mirror IDB
  writes — previously left behind on logout.

Tests:

- `auth-store.test.ts`: cover logout's local-first cleanup path when the
  server call rejects, and assert the sentinel is armed.
- `auth-token.test.ts`: cover the new `endLocalSession` vs
  `clearStoredAuth` split, sentinel honouring in `hydrateStoredAuth`,
  sentinel override of a logged-in injected boot global, and the IDB
  localStorage fallback being wiped.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop logout landing on a black-screen redirect loop

rawCommits.subject: feat(desktop): expose AURA_DESKTOP_EXTERNAL_HARNESS in runtime config
rawCommits.body:
Surface the env var alongside the other harness toggles so the UI can
reflect whether an external harness is being used without reaching into
the process environment itself.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: expose AURA_DESKTOP_EXTERNAL_HARNESS in runtime config

rawCommits.subject: refactor(live-heuristics): drop unused run_id field from LiveAnalyzer
rawCommits.body:
The run_id was stored on the analyzer but never read from it — callers
pass the run_id directly when building the emitted event payload. Drop
the field and tighten the constructor to a zero-arg `new()`, and update
the dev_loop call-site to use the constructor directly with
`get_or_insert_with`.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: drop unused run_id field from LiveAnalyzer

rawCommits.subject: style: apply cargo fmt across server, analyze, and heuristics
rawCommits.body:
No functional changes — just line reflow from `cargo fmt` across the
server handlers, aura-run-analyze CLI, and aura-run-heuristics rules.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: style: apply cargo fmt across server, analyze, and heuristics

Remove unused useIsWriting binding, MARKER_RUN_RE constant, and

rendered.title: Autonomous dev-loop recovery lands, plus a calmer chat and debug surface
rendered.intro: Today's nightly is anchored by a seven-phase push that teaches Aura's dev loop to detect, decompose, and replay tasks that would otherwise fail on truncated writes — backed by live heuristics, remediation hints, and golden tests. Alongside that, the Debug app gets a project-first redesign with a sidekick-driven inspector, chat gains a proper agent-busy state, and a wide sweep of tool-output rendering fixes makes command, file, and list blocks legible again.

entries.title: Autonomous dev-loop recovery and a project-first Debug app
entries.summary: A long overnight push delivered end-to-end autonomous recovery for truncation failures and rebuilt the Debug surface around projects and a sidekick inspector.

items.text: The dev loop can now recover from truncated-write failures on its own: heuristics emit structured remediation hints (split-write, reshape-search, force-tool-call), Phase 3 acts on the first hint by spawning skeleton+fill children or a shaped-retry task, and Phase 5 preemptively decomposes oversized task specs at ingestion — all gated by AURA_AUTO_DECOMPOSE_DISABLED and a per-task opt-out.

A new LiveAnalyzer re-runs heuristics against the in-progress run bundle every 50 events, every 30s, or immediately on task_failed, broadcasting deduped Warn/Error findings as heuristic_finding domain events so the UI can surface advisories mid-flight. A replay integration test and a golden-output fixture pin the full classify → heuristic → preflight decision chain.

The Debug app was rebuilt around projects: the shared LeftMenuTree now lists every project at the top level, and the run toolbar, counters, and entry inspector moved into a sidekick with Run, Events, LLM, Iterations, Blockers, Retries, Stats, and Tasks tabs driven by a new debug-sidekick-store. A portal-backed filter menu stops dropdowns from being clipped, the JSONL envelope is unwrapped so rows show real types and timestamps, and the run-detail header now carries Copy all / Copy filtered and Export buttons.

Chat input now tracks a true agent-busy state: a new useAgentBusy hook combines SSE streaming and loop-active signals so the stop icon appears whenever either source holds the turn, onStop routes to /loop/stop when the automation is the one running, and the server returns a typed 409 agent_busy instead of echoing the raw harness error.

Colored CLI output (cargo, rustc, npm) now renders as readable text in the task panel — the base64 decoder no longer bails on ESC bytes and strips ANSI escapes — and loop_log's token counters stop double-counting mid-stream usage frames, with a new narration_deltas signal added for downstream heuristics.

Visual polish across chat-adjacent surfaces: the leaderboard's stray horizontal scrollbar is gone, and run/task output blocks, sidekick bodies, and preview overlays now share the main chat's subtler border token for a calmer, more consistent outline.

media.reason: Entry looks like a user-visible product change and should receive changelog media.
reasons: entry language points to a visible product surface
entry language points to release plumbing rather than a product screen
media.alt: Autonomous dev-loop recovery and a project-first Debug app screenshot

entries.title: Unified border token and a feed commit-count fallback
entries.summary: The darker chat border token was promoted to a global root variable, and feed push cards stopped showing zero commits when metadata was partial.

items.text: Promoted the #17171a border token to :root so tables, blocks, tool rows, message bubbles, preview overlays, task/terminal panels, and sidekick surfaces all inherit the same outline as the main chat — retiring the per-container overrides added earlier in the day.
PushCardBody now falls back to commitIds.length when metadata.commits is missing, so older feed posts no longer display a misleading "0 commits".

media.reason: Entry looks like a user-visible product change and should receive changelog media.

entries.title: Abandoned the AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN shared-secret path
entries.summary: Work-in-progress shared-secret plumbing between aura-os and the harness was dropped in favor of the existing user JWT flow.

items.text: Removed the uncommitted AURA_NODE_AUTH_TOKEN bearer path across HarnessHttpGateway, LocalHarness, app_builder, and the desktop main entry, consolidating harness auth on the already-shipping user JWT flow. AutomatonClient and SwarmHarness auth_token fields remain but are now permanently unused.

entries.title: Tool-output decoding, linear streaming, and a logout fix
entries.summary: Command, file, and list tool blocks now decode their envelopes correctly, the LLM stream renders strictly in arrival order, and logout no longer traps users in a redirect loop.

items.text: CommandBlock, FileBlock, and ListBlock now run tool-result envelopes through a shared decodeCapturedOutput helper: run_command output renders as legible text, read_file contents show up syntax-highlighted instead of as raw JSON, and list_files / find_files / search_code finally return real rows (with search_code matches split into file:line + preview columns).

Live LLM streaming was untangled: text deltas now strictly append to the timeline tail instead of folding back into earlier text items across tools, markdown safety trims dangling * / _ so half-written emphasis no longer flashes under the cursor, and a new isWriting signal keeps the "cooking" indicator hidden while words are actively revealing but visible during thinking, tool calls, and stalls.

Logout no longer lands on a black-screen redirect loop: App.tsx gates its boot-time showShell flag on the live auth state, logout drops the full-page reload and always runs local cleanup even when the server call throws, and a new aura-force-logged-out sentinel prevents the desktop init script's baked-in auth literals from resurrecting a dead session on reload.

Billing email in Team Settings is now read-only and captioned "Tied to your ZERO account", with billing_email dropped from SetBillingRequest — closing a regression where edits that diverged from the ZERO identity booted the org back to a Free plan.

Hardened the changelog media proof capture pipeline with balanced-block JSON extraction, loose-field fallbacks, and expanded test coverage across demo-agent-brief, demo-screenshot-quality, and demo-seed-planner.

entries.title: External harness flag exposed in desktop runtime config
entries.summary: The desktop runtime config now surfaces AURA_DESKTOP_EXTERNAL_HARNESS alongside the other harness toggles.

items.text: Desktop runtime config now reports AURA_DESKTOP_EXTERNAL_HARNESS so the UI can reflect whether an external harness is in use without reading the process environment directly.

entries.title: Post-merge cleanup to unblock the tsc build
entries.summary: Small follow-ups tightened the LiveAnalyzer constructor and removed unused declarations so the interface TypeScript build passes.

items.text: Dropped the unused run_id field from LiveAnalyzer (callers already pass it at emit time) and removed leftover useIsWriting, MARKER_RUN_RE, and clearStoredAuth declarations so `tsc -b && vite build` completes cleanly.
